sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: Trane Technologies는 2026년 1분기 견조한 실적을 달성하며 연간 매출 및 EPS 가이던스를 상향 조정했습니다.

keyPoints.text: 2026년 1분기 유기적 수주가 24% 증가했으며, 특히 미주 상업용 HVAC 부문이 약 40% 성장하며 실적을 견인했습니다.
기록적인 107억 달러 규모의 수주 잔고를 확보하여 2026년 이후의 강력한 사업 가시성을 확보했습니다.
실적 모멘텀을 바탕으로 연간 매출 및 EPS 가이던스를 상향 조정했습니다.

businessChanges.text: 미주 상업용 HVAC 부문의 강력한 수요에 힘입어 전체 수주 잔고가 전년 말 대비 30% 이상 증가했습니다.
전사적 수주-매출 비율(Book-to-bill)이 135%를 기록하며 글로벌 상업용 HVAC 시장의 높은 수요를 입증했습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 1분기 보고 매출은 50억 달러로 전년 동기 대비 6% 증가했으며, 유기적 매출은 3% 성장했습니다.
조정 주당순이익(EPS)은 2.63달러로 전년 동기 대비 7% 증가했습니다.

watchItems.text: 상향 조정된 연간 가이던스를 달성하기 위한 하반기 영업 마진 개선 여부와 비용 관리 효율성을 확인할 필요가 있습니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: EX-99.1 2 exhibit991-q12026earningsr.htm EX-99.1 Document Trane Technologies Reports Strong First Quarter Results; Raises Full-Year Revenue and EPS Guidance Highlights (first-quarter 2026 versus first-quarter 2025, unless otherwise noted): l Organic bookings* up 24 percent, led by Americas Commercial HVAC, up approximately 40 percent l Record backlog of $10.7 billion, up over 30 percent versus year-end 2025; Americas Commercial HVAC up over 40 percent l Reported revenues of $5 billion, up 6 percent; organic revenues* up 3 percent l GAAP continuing EPS of $2.66; adjusted continuing EPS* of $2.63, up 7 percent * This news release contains non-GAAP financial measures. Definitions of the non-GAAP financial measures can be found in the footnotes of this news r  Normalized numeric aliases: $10.7 billion = 107억 달러; $5 billion = 50억 달러; $10.7 = 10.7달러; $5 = 5달러; $2.66 = 2.66달러; $2.63 = 2.63달러; 24 percent = 24%; 40 percent = 40%; 6 percent = 6%; 30 percent = 30%; 3 percent = 3%; 7 percent = 7%.

:TT), a global climate innovator, today reported diluted earnings per share (EPS) from continuing operations of $2.66 for the first quarter of 2026. Adjusted continuing EPS was $2.63, up 7 percent. First-Quarter 2026 Results Financial Comparisons - First-Quarter Continuing Operations $, millions except EPS Q1 2026 Q1 2025 Y-O-Y Change Organic Y-O-Y Change Bookings $6,691 $5,283 27% 24% Net Revenues $4,969 $4,688 6% 3% GAAP Operating Income $776 $819 (5)% GAAP Operating Margin 15.6% 17.5% (190) bps Adjusted Operating Income* $795 $760 5% Adjusted Operating Margin* 16.0% 16.2% (20) bps Adjusted EBITDA* $881 $851 4% Adjusted EBITDA Margin* 17.7% 18.1% (40) bps GAA  Normalized numeric aliases: $2.66 = 2.66달러; $2.63 = 2.63달러; $6,691 = 6,691달러; $5,283 = 5,283달러; $4,969 = 4,969달러; $4,688 = 4,688달러; $776 = 776달러; $819 = 819달러; $760 = 760달러; $55.2 = 55.2달러; 7 percent = 7%; 6,691 million = 66억 9,100만 달러; 5,283 million = 52억 8,300만 달러; 4,969 million = 49억 6,900만 달러; 4,688 million = 46억 8,800만 달러.

ptional demand for our sustainable products and services,” said Dave Regnery, Chair and CEO, Trane Technologies. “Enterprise bookings grew 24 percent, led by nearly 40 percent growth in our Americas Commercial HVAC business. We closed the quarter with a record $10.7 billion backlog, up more than 30 percent from year-end, giving us strong visibility for 2026 and beyond. With this momentum, our talented global team and consistent execution of our strategy, we’re confident in raising our full-year revenue and EPS guidance and well‑positioned to continue delivering differentiated -more- -2- results. We are energized by the opportunities ahead to advance our purpose and create value for all stakeholders.” Highlights from the First Quarter of 2026 (all comparisons against first-quarter 2025 unless otherwise noted) •  Normalized numeric aliases: $10.7 billion = 107억 달러; $6.7 billion = 67억 달러; $10.7 = 10.7달러; $6.7 = 6.7달러; 24 percent = 24%; 40 percent = 40%; 30 percent = 30%; 27 percent = 27%.

. • Book-to-bill was 135 percent, led by global Commercial HVAC. • Enterprise reported revenues were up 6 percent; organic revenues were up 3 percent. • GAAP operating margin was down 190 basis points, adjusted operating margin was down 20 basis points and adjusted EBITDA margin was down 40 basis points. First-Quarter Business Review (all comparisons against first-quarter 2025 unless otherwise noted) Americas Segment: innovates for customers in the North America and Latin America regions.
